title: SpaceX IPO Draws More Than $100 Billion in Retail Orders
summary: Retail investors bidding for shares in SpaceX’s initial public offering have submitted more than $100 billion in orders, according to people familiar with the matter, as the potentially record-breaking debut enters the home stretch.

title: Novo’s Wegovy Pill Wins Approval in UK for Obesity Treatment
summary: Novo Nordisk A/S’s Wegovy pill was approved in the UK, opening up the obesity market to people who are wary of taking a shot.

summary: BlackRock Inc. is seeking to buy shares in SpaceX’s initial public offering and is targeting an amount of about $5 billion, according to people familiar with the matter, as investors jockey for allocations in what’s expected to be the biggest listing ever.

title: Tech Disruptors: Apollo on Funding the AI Infrastructure Boom
summary: “It’s clear to us that the world is short compute right now, and the industry is racing to catch up,” Rob Bittencourt, partner and head of thematic investing at Apollo, tells Bloomberg Intelligence’s Alexandra Davidov and Paul Gulberg on this episode of the Tech Disruptors podcast. Bittencourt discusses why AI is becoming a private-credit story, with trillions of dollars of data-center, power, chip and infrastructure investment needed to support the next phase of adoption. title: Vanguards of Health Care: Restor3D Personalizes Orthopedics
summary: “If you plan, the cases can go much more smoothly. And if you have patient-specific instrumentation, they can go much more quickly” says restor3D’s CEO Kurt Jacobus in this episode of Vanguards of Healthcare. title: Heat Pumps and the Economics of Clean Heating (Podcast)
summary: Heat pumps are at a crossroads. After a decade of growth, global sales fell in 2025 as weaker construction activity weighed on key markets. Yet the longer-term picture remains resilient. Heat pumps have continued to gain ground against fossil fuel heating in several regions, while Europe remains a focal point for adoption, shaped by a shifting mix of subsidies, energy prices and consumer demand. So what is driving heat pump uptake today, and what will determine whether electrified heating can mo

title: Latin America Currencies Outpace Peers in Fragile Rebound
summary: Emerging-market currencies trimmed losses on Thursday, with Latin American names outperforming peers as falling US Treasury yields helped improve sentiment despite persistent geopolitical concerns.

title: Stocks Get AI Boost in Countdown to SpaceX Debut: Markets Wrap
summary: Wall Street traders gearing up for SpaceX’s historic debut sent stocks higher ahead of what’s expected to be a major test of investor appetite for artificial intelligence-related companies.

title: Wall Street Looks for Proof of AI Profit
summary: Franklin Templeton Senior Investment Strategist Katrina Dudley joined Bloomberg Open Interest to explain why Wall Street is no longer rewarding AI spending without proof of profits. She compares SpaceX's massive investments to the railroad boom, and argues that AI is enhancing worker productivity rather than eliminating jobs. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Veja os Assuntos que Marcaram a Semana no Brasil
summary: Confira o que foi destaque da semana no Brasil com Beatriz Reis. Edição de Patricia Xavier e produção de Barbara Nascimento e Meg Lopes.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Citigroup Seeks $5 Billion From Year’s First High-Grade Bonds
summary: Citigroup Inc. is seeking to raise about $5 billion from its first investment-grade bond sale of 2026, according to people with direct knowledge of the matter.

title: Gold Swings in Volatile Session After Fresh US-Iran Strikes
summary: Gold fluctuated after hitting the lowest since November, following a fresh round of US strikes against Iran that raised the stakes in a war that has roiled global markets and stoked inflation.

title: SpaceX IPO Could Test Market, Says 1789 Capital’s Abrahimzadeh
summary: The public debut of SpaceX is poised to become a major test of investor appetite and market infrastructure, according to 1789 Capital partner Paul Abrahimzadeh, whose firm counts the company as its largest portfolio holding.

title: Oracle Bonds Rally as It Signals Disciplined Funding Outlook
summary: Oracle Corp. bonds rallied on Thursday even as its shares tumbled, after the database giant calmed debt investors by saying it doesn’t expect additional bond issuance this calendar year and outlining plans to raise $40 billion of debt and equity in the current fiscal year.

title: N.O.R.E. & DJ EFN Want To Become the Def Jam of Podcasts
summary: After hitting 500 episodes, Rapper N.O.R.E & DJ EFN, hosts of the show ‘Drink Champs,' discuss what they wanted from the show when it first started, and how it came to be. The two discuss their music careers, hip-hop culture, and their experience navigating the business side of it all. They speak with Romaine Bostick on “The Close.” (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Oil Swings in Thin Trade as US Threatens Key Iranian Export Hub
summary: Oil flipped between gains and losses in thin trading as traders weighed US President Donald Trump’s threats to step up attacks on key Iranian energy infrastructure.

title: What Will Be The Legacy of the 2026 World Cup?
summary: What is the legacy of the 2026 World Cup: expensive tickets or finally growing soccer in the US? On this week's episode of The Deal, Jason Kelly and Alex Rodriguez asked The Athletic's Henry Bushnell if ticket prices will ultimately drown out everything else. Listen to the full interview wherever you get your podcasts. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: ECB Likely Needs to Keep Hiking After Thursday’s Move, IMF Says
summary: The European Central Bank will need to raise borrowing costs again, the International Monetary Fund said just hours after officials in Frankfurt delivered a quarter-point hike.

title: ECB Officials See Next Rate Increase Possible as Soon as July
summary: European Central Bank officials aren’t ruling out a second increase in interest rates as soon as their next monetary-policy meeting, according to people familiar with the situation.

title: US Stocks Gain, Halting Two-Day Drop, as Chip Bellwethers Rise
summary: US stocks edged higher following two straight sessions of declines, as investors assessed developments in the Middle East and a report that showed US producer prices climbed at the fastest pace in more than three years.

title: Bloomberg Surveillance 6/11/2026
summary: Jonathan Ferro, Lisa Abramowicz and Annmarie Hordern speak daily with leaders and decision makers from Wall Street to Washington and beyond. No other program better positions investors and executives for the trading day. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Your ETF of the Month: Software Isn’t Dead Yet
summary: This fund proves that the industry’s demise at the hands of artificial intelligence has been somewhat exaggerated.

title: Why Tomatoes Are the Most Expensive They’ve Been in Four Decades
summary:
In April, the price of tomatoes was around $2.69 per pound — the highest seen in some four decades. So what's driving the price spike?

Jacob Krempel, senior vice president of procurement and merchandising at the wholesale food distributor Baldor, joins Tracy Alloway and Joe Weisenthal to unpack why consumers are paying more and more at the grocery store. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: SEC Proposes to Scrap Trade-Through Rule for Equities Execution
summary: The Securities and Exchange Commission proposed eliminating a rule that aims to ensure investors get better prices for their transactions.

title: Supreme Court Shields Investment Funds From Some Shareholder Suits
summary: The US Supreme Court shielded funds from some investor lawsuits in a 6-3 vote, blocking activist investors from suing 11 closed-end funds. Tyler Kendall reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: AI Boom Stokes Inflation With Memory Chips at ‘Insane’ Prices
summary: When Chris Barber, who’s been in the computer business for a quarter-century, says he’s never seen anything like the AI boom, he’s not talking about its potential to transform the future. He means what it’s doing to hardware prices right now.

title: Treasuries Rise on Lower Oil Prices, Mixed Inflation Readings
summary: Treasuries rose as a drop in oil prices improved the outlook for US inflation.

title: Oracle Falls on Data Center Spending; Hugo Boss, Stitch Fix Rally | Stock Movers
summary:
On this episode of Stock Movers with Alexis Christoforous:

- Oracle (ORCL) shares are lower after the company reported quarterly capital expenses that were higher than estimates. The company expects to spend about $70 billion on net capital expenditures in the current fiscal year, which ends in May 2027.

- Hugo Boss (BOSS) shares are moving on news Frasers Group Plc offered to buy the rest of Hugo Boss AG for about €2 billion ($2.3 billion) as billionaire Mike Ashley seeks to add another fashion brand to his growing collection.

- Stitch Fix (SFIX) shares are moving as it bumped up its fiscal-year outlook after revenue rose in its latest quarter, citing a boost from efforts to improve its customer experience and assortment. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: SpaceX Treated as ‘Simply Too Risky’ for Funds With Governance Mandates
summary: The list of sustainability-focused fund managers opting to blacklist SpaceX is growing, as they contemplate the unprecedented level of control that Elon Musk will hold over the rockets-to-chatbot behemoth.

title: Europe Set to Get Rare Oman, UAE Oil as China Curbs Purchases
summary: A wave of oil from the Middle East is set to arrive in Europe next month, a tangible example of how a collapse in Chinese imports is helping other countries to cope with the unprecedented supply disruption wrought by the Iran war.

title: Private Markets on Asia Conviction
summary: Jenny Lee, Senior Managing Partner, Granite Asia and Mingchen Xia, Managing Director & Co-Head of Asia Investments, Hamilton Lane struck a bullish tone on the private credit market speaking with Bloomberg’s Lulu Chen at Bloomberg Invest 2026 in Hong Kong.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Zambia’s Bond Buyback Hands More Impetus to 39% Local-Debt Rally
summary: Zambia’s world-beating local debt may be poised for further gains after the government’s success buying back a potentially costly dollar bond.

title: SpaceX Valuation Is Cheap for Space Peers But Pricey as AI Stock
summary: The jury is still out on whether SpaceX is primarily a rocket company, as its name suggests, or actually more of a telecom provider or artificial intelligence play. Its expected valuation doesn’t help resolve the confusion.
